title: Case Unknown
description: An interactive detective game with a chatbot interface.
longDescription: Case Unknown is an interactive storytelling experience that challenges players to outsmart a cynical AI detective. Using a mix of memory fragments and strategic dialogue, players navigate a high-stakes interrogation designed with a time pressure element to increase tension. This project allowed me to explore the intersection of Generative AI and immersive game design, focusing on creating a cohesive, moody atmosphere.

title: The REM Project
description: An interactive Three.js escape room game exploring the boundary between dreams and reality.
longDescription: The REM Projekt is an immersive 3D experience developed as part of a collaborative university module. Set in a surreal dreamland, players have to solve interactive puzzle chains. The game uses a modular architecture to blend narrative cutscenes with real-time gameplay. It explores themes of sleep deprivation and subconscious unrest through a unique and dreamy aesthetic, utilizing advanced Three.js features like custom raycasting, spatial audio, and dynamic environmental effects to create a hauntingly atmospheric journey. The game is available on HuggingFace Spaces (Link below).

title: Franz.
description: A full-stack marketplace prototype built to explore cybersecurity vulnerabilities.
longDescription: Franz. is a university project designed as a hands-on cybersecurity laboratory. We developed a prototype full-stack secondhand marketplace to actively explore how security flaws manifest in real-world applications. By intentionally implementing and then exploiting vulnerabilities such as Broken Access Control, Sensitive Data Exposure, and Injection issues, we gained deep insights into defensive coding. The project involved building everything from the ground up—including a modular JavaScript frontend, a Flask-based service layer, and a persistent SQLite database—to simulate a complete attack surface for educational testing.

title: Let's Move
description: A data archive exploring 70 months of personal athletic history.
longDescription: Let’s Move is a deep dive into five years of personal fitness data, transforming over 2,100 days of activity into a cohesive visual narrative. The project navigates through different scales of data—from a macro-overview of a 70-month training journey to the micro-anatomy of a single run. Built with a futuristic, minimalist aesthetic, it features an interactive grid system and a synchronized biometrics dashboard. This project explores the intersection of information design and personal storytelling, highlighting how global events like the pandemic lockdowns created unique, visible peaks in individual behavioral data.

title: Laberfactory
description: A responsive Svelte web experience for a German podcast.
longDescription: Laberfactory is a modern podcast website built in Svelte. It includes a dynamic episodes tab, a news section, and smooth section snapping.
